# Reference test fixtures

Three kinds live here, distinguished by provenance — not by a fixed cap:

- **`golden/` real-sanitized** — operator-generated by piping a real
  intervals.icu response through `tools/sanitize-fixture.ts` (default-deny
  allowlist transform). Realistic API-shape variation, including TP-trademark
  fields that ride through `z.looseObject()` and stay off the typed surface.

- **`golden/` real-derived hybrid** — sanitized real rows PLUS synthetic
  blocks attached after the sanitizer. `curve-equipped.json` is the example:
  the schema-derived default-deny sanitizer would drop every curve key and its
  id transform would clobber the `r.<start>.<end>` curve ids, so
  `tools/build-curve-fixture.ts` sanitizes the activity/wellness rows, then
  attaches `power_curves` / `hr_curves` / `sustainability_curves` /
  `athlete.sportSettings` with ids generated from the frozen clock.

- **`golden/` synthetic** + **`synthetic/`** — hand-crafted fixtures encoding a
  specific regression or boundary case. The boundary fixtures are fuzz-derived
  (a `fuzz-parity` divergence frozen as a golden). `synthetic/` fixtures ship
  with a `_comment` key explaining the bug they lock in (JSON has no comment
  syntax, so the comment rides through `z.looseObject()`). `dfa-equipped.json`
  is a fully-synthetic golden (zero sanitizer involvement, zero real data): its
  per-second stream blob is generated entirely by `tools/build-dfa-fixture.ts`
  from closed-form segment patterns.

## Current fixtures

The snapshot harness (`tools/snapshot-section-11.ts`) processes the fixtures
listed in `HARNESS_FIXTURES`; the coverage column names the sync.py branch each
one newly covers (the justification the harness fixture registry records).

| File | Kind | sync.py branch newly covered |
|---|---|---|
| `golden/realistic-athlete.json` | real-sanitized | Populated branch of every load/recovery metric — 12 weeks of activities + wellness + ftp_history |
| `golden/curve-equipped.json` | real-derived hybrid | `_calculate_power_curve_delta` + `_calculate_hr_curve_delta` (both 28d windows, all rotation anchors → non-null `rotation_index`); `_calculate_sustainability_profile` (cycling block: actual_watts/actual_hr/pct_lthr + Coggan + CP/W' model layers); `_extract_power_model_from_wellness` + the 6 power-model scalars |
| `golden/new-athlete-empty.json` | synthetic | Every "no data" branch — ACWR div-by-zero, monotony of an empty week, recovery_index no-contributors |
| `golden/data-gap-mid-history.json` | synthetic | EWMA decay through a 28d gap; ACWR chronic window seeing zeros |
| `golden/boundary-monotony.json` | synthetic (fuzz) | monotony mean/stdev on the 2-dp rounding boundary (defends the exact-rational stdev port) |
| `golden/boundary-sum-strain.json` | synthetic (fuzz) | Neumaier-compensated daily-load sum on the rounding boundary (defends the compensated-sum port) |
| `golden/boundary-zone-total-secs.json` | synthetic (fuzz) | per-activity compensated zone-total summation on a 1-ULP boundary |
| `golden/multisport-tie.json` | synthetic | primary-sport strict-`>` tiebreak (`max(dict, key=dict.get)` insertion order) |
| `golden/multisport-thin-primary.json` | synthetic | `effective_monotony` fallback when primary-sport monotony is null (<3 active days) |
| `golden/populated-benchmark-and-consistency.json` | synthetic | populated `consistency_index` + `benchmark_indoor`/`benchmark_outdoor` (the +/-7d FTP nearest-match window) |
| `golden/rest-week-with-baseline.json` | synthetic | monotony `stdev<=0 → null` cascade (strain/stress_tolerance null) + flat HRV/RHR baseline block |
| `golden/capability-qualifying.json` | real-derived hybrid | `_calculate_durability` reliability gate (N7>=3, N28>=5) + efficiency_factor + HRRc means/trends — sanitized realistic-athlete base + 5 synthetic qualifying Rides appended at the tail |
| `golden/dfa-equipped.json` | synthetic | `_compute_dfa_block` + `_calculate_dfa_a1_profile` populated branch: 7 Ride sessions with per-second `dfa_a1`/`artifacts`/`heartrate`/`watts` streams clearing valid_secs>=1200 ∧ valid_pct>=70 and >=60s dwell in BOTH crossing bands → cycling trailing window at confidence=high with non-null lt1/lt2 estimates |
| `synthetic/has-intervals-placeholder.json` | synthetic | upstream v3.106 bug: a `RECOVERY`-only `icu_intervals` placeholder must NOT be flagged as a structured workout |

Fixtures owned by other test suites (`golden/zero-activities.json`,
`golden/post-break-resume.json`) live here but are intentionally excluded from
`HARNESS_FIXTURES` — they don't conform to sync.py's input contract.

## Adding a new fixture

For a new **real-sanitized golden**, run the sanitize CLI:

```bash
pnpm exec tsx tools/sanitize-fixture.ts <input.json> <output-name> --force
```

The CLI emits a summary listing dropped TP-trademark + PII keys so you can
confirm the default-deny transform ran, and writes a `.sha256` checksum
alongside the JSON.

For a new **real-derived hybrid**, use a dedicated builder so the synthetic
blocks bypass the sanitizer correctly. `tools/build-curve-fixture.ts` attaches
synthetic curve blocks to sanitized real rows; `tools/build-capability-fixture.ts`
appends synthetic qualifying Rides to the sanitized realistic-athlete base.

```bash
pnpm exec tsx tools/build-curve-fixture.ts --raw-bundle <bundle.json> --raw-curves <curves.json>
pnpm exec tsx tools/build-capability-fixture.ts
```

For a new **synthetic** fixture, add the file by hand, include a `_comment` key
explaining the regression case, and write a test that asserts the offending
behavior is now blocked. Then register it in `HARNESS_FIXTURES` (with its own
`frozenNow`) if it should get a full per-metric oracle capture. A
**fully-synthetic stream fixture** (large generated blob) gets its own builder
instead — `tools/build-dfa-fixture.ts` generates `dfa-equipped.json`
deterministically and ends with a non-vacuity guard that recomputes the
sufficiency + crossing-band thresholds, so the capture can't be silently
vacuous.

## Integrity + privacy guards

- **`.sha256` checksum** — `realistic-athlete`, `curve-equipped`,
  `dfa-equipped`, and `capability-qualifying` each carry a committed checksum;
  `realistic-athlete-fixture-checksum.test.ts` re-hashes the bytes on every CI
  run, catching in-place mutation (bad merge, editor save).
- **PII allowlist scan** — `reference-load-fixture.test.ts` walks the
  real-derived + synthetic-stream fixtures and asserts every key is in
  `ALLOWED_FIXTURE_KEYS` (real rows) or the enumerated synthetic-block key set
  (curve-equipped curve/athlete blocks; dfa-equipped stream channels), and that
  every numeric `id`/`*_id` is the redacted sentinel (12345) or a synthetic
  fixture id (curve >= 90101, dfa >= 90201, capability-qualifying Rides >= 90001)
  — never a real-shaped account id. `dfa-equipped` is fully synthetic, so its
  only out-of-allowlist keys are the structural stream-channel names + the
  `String(id)` record keys (>= 90201); `capability-qualifying` adds no
  out-of-allowlist keys (its appended Rides carry only ActivitySchema fields).

## Privacy callout

**NEVER commit a JSON file you didn't pipe through `tools/sanitize-fixture.ts`
(real-sanitized) or `tools/build-curve-fixture.ts` (real-derived hybrid).**

Real intervals.icu responses contain GPS coordinates pinpointing the operator's
home address, email-shaped strings in `notes`/`description`, and other fields
that re-identify the operator. The default-deny sanitizer is the privacy
boundary; bypassing it leaks PII into the public repo. The curve builder runs
the same sanitizer over its real rows and only attaches synthetic (PII-free)
blocks afterward.
